Package nonprofitbookkeeping.plugin

Interface Plugin

All Known Implementing Classes:
SamplePlugin, SCALedgerPlugin
public interface Plugin
Defines the contract for plugins in the nonprofit bookkeeping application. Plugins can extend the application's functionality, for example, by adding new menu items, providing new features, or integrating with external services.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addMenuItems(javafx.scene.control.MenuBar mainMenuBar)
    Allows the plugin to add custom menu items to the application's main menu bar.
    String
    getDescription()
    Gets a brief description of what the plugin does.
    String
    getName()
    Gets the name of the plugin.
    void
    initialize(ApplicationContext context)
    Initializes the plugin with the application context.
    void
    shutdown()
    Called when the application is shutting down or when the plugin is being unloaded.

  • Method Details

    • getName

      String getName()
      Gets the name of the plugin. This name may be displayed in the UI or used for identification.
      Returns:
      The name of the plugin.

    • getDescription

      String getDescription()
      Gets a brief description of what the plugin does. This description can be used in a plugin manager UI or for help purposes.
      Returns:
      A string describing the plugin's functionality.

    • initialize

      void initialize(ApplicationContext context) throws Exception
      Initializes the plugin with the application context. This method is called once when the plugin is loaded by the application. Plugins can use the application context to access core application services and UI components like the primary stage.
      Parameters:
      context - The ApplicationContext providing access to core application functionalities.
      Throws:
      Exception - if an error occurs during plugin initialization.

    • addMenuItems

      void addMenuItems(javafx.scene.control.MenuBar mainMenuBar)
      Allows the plugin to add custom menu items to the application's main menu bar. This method is called after initialize(ApplicationContext) and provides the plugin with a reference to the main MenuBar.
      Parameters:
      mainMenuBar - The main MenuBar of the application to which custom menus or items can be added.

    • shutdown

      void shutdown()
      Called when the application is shutting down or when the plugin is being unloaded. Plugins should use this method to perform any necessary cleanup, such as releasing resources, saving state, or closing connections.
